SUMMONS ON COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF OLGA K., A MINOR BY AND THROUGH HER GUARDIAN AD LITEM ANGELIKA ZIDANE SERVED MAY-13-2011, SUBSTITUTE SERVICE ON NATURAL PERSON AS TO DEFENDANT SECURITY INDUSTRY SPECIALISTS, INC.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ALLIEDBARTON SECURITY SERVICES, LP'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD FACTS TO SHOW THAT DEFENDANT HAD A DUTY OF CARE TO PLAINTIFF (E.G. DUTY TO COMPLY WITH PENAL CODE 847(A)). DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENT SUPERVISION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD FACTS TO SUPPORT HOW DEFENDANT WAS NEGLIGENT IN SUPERVISING ITS OWN GUARD. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PREMISES LIABILITY IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD FACTS TO SHOW THAT DEFENDANT WAS AWARE THAT IT CREATED A DANGEROUS CONDITION WHEN IT'S EMPLOYEE TURNED CUSTODY OF PLAINTIFF OVER TO YNGOJOWANG. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER IS TO THE 11TH CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD FURTHER FACTS TO SUPPORT ITS CLAIM THAT DEFENDANT OWED A DUTY OF CARE TO PLAINTIFF AND THAT PLAINTIFF SUFFERED SERIOUS EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. IN ADDITION, THE COURT INDICATED THAT PLAINTIFF HAS 20 DAYS FROM NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER TO FILE THE AMENDED COMPLAINT. PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER TO BE PRESENTED TO THE COURT. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-16-2011 Continued To Dec-02-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-21-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT ALLIEDBARTON SECURITY SERVICES, LP'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT.

APPEARANCES: Roxanne Irani, Esq. of Haight, Brown & Bonesteel, LLP 714/426-4603 for deft Security Industry Specialists, Inc. (appearing via CourtCall); no other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Defendant's Demurrer to the 1st cause of action for negligence is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead facts to show that Defendant had a duty of care to plaintiff (e.g. duty to comply with Penal Code 847(a)).
Defendant's Demurrer to the 2nd cause of action for negligent supervision is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead facts to support how Defendant was negligent in supervising its own guard.
Defendant's Demurrer to the 4th cause of action for premises liability is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead facts to show that Defendant was aware that it created a dangerous condition when it's employee turned custody of Plaintiff over to Yngojowang.
Defendant's Demurrer is to the 11th cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead further facts to support its claim that Defendant owed a duty of care to Plaintiff and that Plaintiff suffered serious emotional distress.
In addition, the Court indicated that plaintiff has 20 days from notice of entry of order to file the amended complaint. Proposed form of order to be presented to the Court. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
